steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank introduces a sentence that generalizes from the Arkansas and Maine examples: years pass between selection and official designation because the legislative process is slow. The transition must connect the examples to this general statement about frequency.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Often,': Signals that something happens frequently. This fits: years typically pass between selection and designation, and the reason (slow legislature) follows naturally. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Therefore,': Signals a logical conclusion drawn from what came before. But the sentence isn't a conclusion from the examples — it's a general pattern, and the cause (slow legislative process) comes after the blank. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Similarly,': Signals a parallel. But the sentence isn't another example parallel to Arkansas and Maine — it's a general statement explaining them. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Indeed,': Signals emphasis or confirmation. This would work only if the general statement simply restated the examples — but the sentence adds new information (the legislative process reason), so mere emphasis is too weak. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
